What […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> The researcher Microsoft threatened just dropped a seventh Windows zero-day hours after Patch Tuesday https://thenextweb.com/news/chaotic-eclipse-rogueplanet-windows-defender-zero-day Wed, 10 Jun 2026 22:49:45 +0000 TheNextWeb=e88c0fb255926a3f708c22be812b4a37 Chaotic Eclipse, the security researcher Microsoft threatened with criminal prosecution, has published a seventh Windows zero-day exploit. Called RoguePlanet, it grants attackers SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows 10 and 11 machines. The researcher released the proof-of-concept hours after Microsoft shipped its June Patch Tuesday update, which fixed a record 200 vulnerabilities. CEO David Ellison announced the consolidation earlier this year. It is designed to eliminate duplicate systems, improve the app experience, and build the playbook for absorbing HBO Max after the $110 billion Warner Bros. Discovery merger […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Anthropic wants governments to have the power to block dangerous AI deployments https://thenextweb.com/news/anthropic-advanced-ai-framework-government-block-deployments Wed, 10 Jun 2026 22:16:55 +0000 TheNextWeb=cfee7edeb341158110ece5fba9440f58 Anthropic has published two policy frameworks calling for governments to have the legal authority to block dangerous AI deployments and for economic safeguards to protect workers as AI reshapes the labour market. The Advanced AI Framework covers safety regulation. The Economic Policy Framework addresses displacement, capital distribution, and the social safety net. The city council voted 7-0 to expand its contract with Flock Safety, adding a drone-as-first-responder platform to the automatic licence plate readers the company already supplies. Published on Microsoft’s official blog on Tuesday, the essay called the backlash a “powerful wake-up call for the tech sector.” His prescription: the graduates should […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> White House offers to trade state AI preemption for federal online safety laws in new deal with Congress https://thenextweb.com/news/white-house-ai-preemption-kosa-no-fakes-deal Wed, 10 Jun 2026 21:27:44 +0000 TheNextWeb=2709057209cbdf5de3c14e4de17e0642 The White House is negotiating with key senators to bundle federal preemption of state AI laws with three online safety bills, Axios reported. Senator Marsha Blackburn is leading the effort to finalise legislative text. The cash is available through the end of September. Each draw has a three-year repayment window.
